titleEn: Marketing strategies to regain product appeal
titleZh: 梨果营销困境：欧洲各国如何应对年轻消费者流失
summaryZh: 本文报道了Interpera会议上关于梨果营销的讨论，重点分析了欧洲主要梨生产国（意大利、荷兰、比利时、波兰、西班牙等）的出口格局与消费趋势。数据显示，梨果面临年轻消费者流失（荷兰35岁以下消费下降20%）、家庭购买率下滑（10年下降10个百分点）等挑战。各国采取不同策略：荷兰聚焦年轻一代品牌化沟通，法国转向内需市场，波兰依赖非欧盟出口。文章揭示了传统农产品在消费代际更替中的共性危机，但未涉及智慧畜牧或农业科技应用。
featuredReason: 本文揭示了传统农产品（梨）面临的年轻消费者流失和消费代际断层问题，虽未直接涉及智慧畜牧技术，但为农业从业者提供了营销转型和品牌年轻化的思考方向，可启发智慧畜牧领域在消费端的数据洞察与精准营销策略。
contentFull: What do the various producing countries do? Marketing strategies to regain product appeal Selling pears is becoming increasingly difficult due to a lack of young consumers. The second morning session of Interpera (Ferrara, 25 June 2026) focused on marketing. Elisa Macchi (director of the CSO) said that "At European level, Italy has lost market share over the last eight years, while the other countries have remained more or less constant. The export trend has declined, but not as much as production. Italy had never previously exported large quantities of pears. Poland produces over 100,000 tonnes, with almost all of them (97%) exported. The Netherlands exports 86%, whilst Italy exports 14% (it used to export 20% when production was at its peak, up until 2018)." © Cristiano Riciputi | FreshPlaza.comElisa Macchi, Director of the CSO The Netherlands has increased its exports to Italy, Spain and Romania. Belgium exports mainly within the EU. Shipments to the Netherlands, Germany and Italy are on the rise. Non-EU countries export to the UK and the Far East, whilst Spain exports to France, Germany, Italy and the Middle East. Poland exports entirely outside the EU, to Kazakhstan, Belarus and Eastern Europe in general. © Cristiano Riciputi | FreshPlaza.com "Italy and France export very little. In Germany, exports have fallen by over 50%, whilst France has decided to focus on promoting the product on the domestic market. Among the net importing countries, Brazil, the Scandinavian countries and Central Asia are worth noting (Poland and China export to these regions). © Cristiano Riciputi | FreshPlaza.comWilco van den Berg (Fresh Produce Centre, Netherlands) It was then the turn of the European testimonials. Wilco van den Berg (Netherlands): "Our country specialises in exports, including re-exports (for example, imports from South Africa and Chile followed by re-export). However, we are still making progress. We ship to Germany, Poland and the United Kingdom (the top three). Dutch pears sell well throughout Europe, but it is not easy to market in China. We used to ship there too until 2014, but it became more difficult after that. Generally speaking, we are exporting more, but there is a decline in pear consumption. In the Netherlands, there has been a considerable drop over the last five years - 20% among the under-35s. How are we going to sell all these pears if people aren't eating them? We need a communication strategy. Consumption needs to be stimulated. Over the past 10 years, we have lost 10% of households that buy pears (from 70 to 60%), with 40% never buying them at all. We need to focus on the younger generations. We must give pears a face, as they are too anonymous on supermarket shelves." © Cristiano Riciputi | FreshPlaza.comSpeaking on behalf of Belgium was Kris Jans of BelOrta Kris Jans (BelOrta): "Despite being a very small country, Belgium has been the EU's leading pear-growing nation for several years now. We export to almost all of Europe. We need to think about exporting worldwide, though it is true that every day brings a surprise, due to the 'whims' of world leaders, with ever-increasing logistical difficulties. We need to diversify the organoleptic profile to make pears more appealing. Why is mango used as an ingredient in cooking, but not pears?" © Cristiano Riciputi | FreshPlaza.comJoan Serentill (Afrucat, Spain) Joan Serentill (Afrucat, Spain): "We export different varieties depending on the destination. Brazil imports Williams pears, for example. In France, we export Williams pears for the processing industry, and it also depends on the size. For instance, large-sized Conference pears are mostly sold on the Spanish domestic market. To southern Italy, we export large-sized Conference pears, which are very juicy and sweet. So it's a matter of specialisation and variety, depending on the country. We are importing more pears from Belgium, especially large-sized Conference pears." © Cristiano Riciputi | FreshPlaza.comVitor Fonseca (ANP) Vitor Fonseca (ANP) spoke on behalf of Portugal. "Brazil is our main market. We do, however, have a problem with Williams pears in Argentina, because their currency is weak. We have customers in various European countries. We supply pears to schools for snacks." © Cristiano Riciputi | FreshPlaza.comBernard Nicolai (ANPP, France) Bernard Nicolai (ANPP, France): "The French domestic market has responded well. We face a slight difficulty at the end of summer, but the end of the season went well. We need to address the issue of consumption and find new strategies to boost it. There are new varieties gaining ground. We export less than 10,000 tonnes - very little, but we should be doing more." © Cristiano Riciputi | FreshPlaza.comManuel Manfredi (Unapera, Italy) Manuel Manfredi (Unapera), speaking on behalf of Italy, stated that "We have focused on safeguarding the value of varieties such as Abate and Williams. This has worked well for larger sizes, whilst we have struggled with the smaller ones and second-grade fruit. Williams has delivered better yields in recent years. For the coming season, I remain very cautious and prudent, as harvesting is still a long way away, particularly for the autumn varieties. However, the recovery will not be structural. We will continue to try to defend value and attempt to regain some market share in foreign countries. Defending prices alone, however, is not enough to offset the production shortfall." translationZh:
各生产国在做什么？重获产品吸引力的营销策略
由于缺乏年轻消费者，梨的销售变得越来越困难。Interpera（费拉拉，2026年6月25日）第二天上午的会议聚焦于营销。CSO总监Elisa Macchi表示：“在欧洲层面，意大利在过去八年中失去了市场份额，而其他国家则基本保持不变。出口趋势有所下降，但降幅不及产量。意大利此前从未大量出口梨。波兰产量超过10万吨，几乎全部（97%）用于出口。荷兰出口86%，而意大利出口14%（在2018年之前的产量高峰期，意大利曾出口20%）。”
© Cristiano Riciputi | FreshPlaza.com
CSO总监Elisa Macchi
荷兰增加了对意大利、西班牙和罗马尼亚的出口。比利时主要向欧盟内部出口。对荷兰、德国和意大利的出货量正在上升。非欧盟国家向英国和远东地区出口，而西班牙则向法国、德国、意大利和中东出口。波兰完全向欧盟以外出口，目的地包括哈萨克斯坦、白俄罗斯和东欧地区。
© Cristiano Riciputi | FreshPlaza.com
“意大利和法国出口很少。在德国，出口下降了50%以上，而法国则决定专注于在国内市场推广产品。在净进口国中，巴西、斯堪的纳维亚国家和中亚值得注意（波兰和中国向这些地区出口）。”
© Cristiano Riciputi | FreshPlaza.com
荷兰Fresh Produce Centre的Wilco van den Berg
随后轮到欧洲代表发言。荷兰的Wilco van den Berg表示：“我们国家专攻出口，包括转口（例如，从南非和智利进口后再出口）。不过，我们仍在取得进展。我们向德国、波兰和英国（前三名）发货。荷兰梨在整个欧洲销售良好，但在中国市场销售并不容易。2014年之前我们也曾向那里发货，但之后变得困难。总体而言，我们的出口量在增加，但梨的消费量却在下降。在荷兰，过去五年中，35岁以下人群的消费量下降了20%。如果人们不吃梨，我们怎么卖掉所有这些梨？我们需要一个沟通策略。消费需要被刺激。过去10年中，我们失去了10%购买梨的家庭（从70%降至60%），而40%的家庭从未购买过梨。我们需要关注年轻一代。我们必须让梨拥有面孔，因为它们在超市货架上太匿名了。”
© Cristiano Riciputi | FreshPlaza.com
代表比利时发言的是BelOrta的Kris Jans
Kris Jans（BelOrta）：“尽管比利时是一个非常小的国家，但多年来它一直是欧盟领先的梨种植国。我们几乎向整个欧洲出口。”
contentHtml: Selling pears is becoming increasingly difficult due to a lack of young consumers. The second morning session of Interpera (Ferrara, 25 June 2026) focused on marketing. Elisa Macchi (director of the CSO) said that "At European level, Italy has lost market share over the last eight years, while the other…
